threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 48 bus runs on time and aren't yet aware it has a mind of its own. Good luck with that!
On the 415 last night, a guy serenaded the bus with classic soul. Everyone pretended to be asleep but a few were nodding along.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a couple arguing in Turkish while their toddler calmly fed pigeons; that’s when I knew this place isn't just a town, it's a world.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here can you catch a bus that stops in the middle of a street party. BVG drivers nodding to DJs and the whole vibe just keeps on rolling.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid the Civic Center BART on a Friday—loud bangs and smoke are just part of our commute now.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just watched a Muni bus inch around a yoga class spilling out onto the street—people dodging toward the next namaste session instead of the stop.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists assume all Gaudí buildings are in the same spot. They miss the hidden charm of the small, colorful houses tucked away in our local streets.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them lingering too long at the ticket machines in metro stations, staring like it’s a puzzle, missing the crowd flow completely.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can catch a bus on a Sunday without planning an hour ahead. The locals know you’re in for a long wait.
Caught a glimpse of a busker playing a clarinet by the Peckham Rye stop last night. Must've been 2 AM, and he had a little crowd dancing. Only in South London.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a group of kids playing football on the street while their parents chatted over beers. That simple mix of chaos and community made it feel like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you sip a beer on the S-Bahn while the train rattles through abandoned factories. It's a moving museum of urban decay and culture.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively avoid the BART Civic Center on Fridays. Not just for the crowds, but the underlying chaos they know too well.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 5-Fulton halted for 20 minutes because a guy set up a chess game in the aisle. Only in SF would a bus become a tournament hall.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think all locals sip coffee at fancy cafés, but you'll find most of us jostling for space at the bar in our neighborhood bodegas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their enthusiasm for brunch; they still think a café con leche is a breakfast miracle rather than just a daily necessity.
